rationale: The biomarkers can be captured directly through bodily responses or single-level behaviors.
The self-report, functional status and pain can reflect bodily state.
The behaviors such as  exercise, adherence and sleep are clear actions, and the status can generally be understood through self-report.

domain_desc: Depression / anxiety / negative affect / general distress
rationale: The severity of mental/emotional distress, such as depression and anxiety, can be evaluated using a single scale at each time point.

domain_desc: Intrusive thoughts / avoidance / hyperarousal / maladaptive schemas
rationale: The symptons such as avoidance can be measured primarily on a single dimension and do not require responses across multiple life domains.

domain_desc: Well-being / life satisfaction / positive affect / flourishing
rationale: The condition of well-being and life satisfaction are socially related and require further clarification across different social contexts and backgrounds.

domain_desc: Relationship quality / social support / social adjustment
rationale: Relationship quality and social support require more information from multiple scenarios.

domain_desc: Post-traumatic growth / benefit finding / sense of coherence / coping self-efficacy
rationale: The post-traumatic growth requires further evaluation across multiple dimensions, such as narrative and cognition.
